dublinCore.dc:title: 86 S4789 IS: Blue Star Mothers Day Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Ernst introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend chapter 1 of title 36, United States Code, to designate February 1 of each year as Blue Star Mothers Day.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Blue Star Mothers Day Act.

paragraph.text: On February 1, 1942, mothers of members of the Armed Forces gathered in Flint, Michigan, marking the founding of what would become Blue Star Mothers of America, Inc.

Blue Star Mothers of America, Inc., was chartered by Congress under the Act entitled An Act for the incorporation of the Blue Star Mothers of America, Inc. (Public Law 86–653; 74 Stat. 515) in recognition of its role in supporting military families and promoting patriotic service.
Blue Star Mothers make significant sacrifices while supporting sons and daughters serving in the Armed Forces.
Through volunteer service and support for servicemembers, veterans, and military families, Blue Star Mothers continue to strengthen military communities across the United States.

subsection.text: Chapter 1 of title 36, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:
February 1 is Blue Star Mothers Day.

All private citizens, organizations, and Federal, State, and local governmental and legislative entities are encouraged to recognize Blue Star Mothers Day through proclamations, activities, and educational efforts honoring the service, sacrifice, and contributions of Blue Star Mothers.
The table of sections for chapter 1 of title 36, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:
